steps: Disconnect the dishwasher from power.
Pull out the lower dish rack.
Locate the damaged rack wheel.
Snap off the old wheel from the rack.
Snap the new rack wheel into place.
Reinstall the rack and restore power.
Disconnect power to the dishwasher.
Turn off the water supply.
Remove the lower access panel.
Locate the drain pump assembly.
Disconnect the hoses and electrical connector.
Remove the pump and install the new drain pump.
Reconnect hoses and restore power.
Open the dishwasher and remove the lower rack.
Locate the spray arm at the bottom.
Twist or unscrew the spray arm to remove it.
Install the new spray arm.
Reinsert the rack and test operation.
Disconnect power to the dishwasher.
Open the dishwasher door.
Remove screws securing the inner door panel.
Locate the door latch assembly.
Disconnect wiring and remove the latch.
Install the new latch and reassemble the door.
Disconnect power to the dishwasher.
Remove the lower dish rack.
Unscrew and disconnect the heating element terminals.
Remove the heating element from the tub.
Install the new heating element and reconnect wires.
Reassemble and test the dishwasher.
Disconnect power to the dishwasher.
Remove the lower rack.
Locate the float switch under the float assembly.
Disconnect the wiring harness.
Remove the switch and install the new one.
Reconnect wiring and restore power.
Disconnect power to the refrigerator.
Remove the ice bin from the freezer.
Unscrew the mounting screws securing the ice maker.
Disconnect the wiring harness.
Install the new ice maker assembly.
Reconnect wiring and reinstall mounting screws.
Disconnect power to the refrigerator.
Turn off the household water supply.
Pull the refrigerator away from the wall.
Locate the water inlet valve at the back.
Disconnect water lines and electrical connectors.
Install the new valve and reconnect lines.
Disconnect power to the refrigerator.
Remove the rear freezer panel.
Locate the evaporator fan motor.
Disconnect wiring harness.
Remove mounting screws and replace the fan motor.
Reinstall panel and restore power.
Open the refrigerator door.
Pull the old door gasket away from the door liner.
Align the new gasket along the door edges.
Press the gasket into the groove around the door.
Ensure the gasket seals evenly.
